My task was to design a visually consistent, clear, and conversion-oriented page that communicates the program rules and user benefits in an accessible way.

absolvent-agency.title: Employer Branding Agency
absolvent-agency.description: Design project new Absolvent Consulting’s website

moreSections.heading: This is not my one and only case

absolvent.heading: Employer Branding Agency
absolvent.intro: Absolvent Consulting is an employer branding agency with several years of experience in the industry. A creative team of professionals in their fields. They work with major brands on a daily basis, and their creations and ideas can be found every day on social media and the internet

challenge.heading: Challenge
challenge.description: Absolvent Consulting encountered an unpleasant situation. The website redesign project had already been going on for a year because the original contractor disappeared at a certain stage of the work, and the project had to be completed

goal.heading: Goal
goal.description: Auditing current materials and sifting out items that were no longer suitable for further use. Completing the project

audit.title: Audit and structuring of the existing project

ux-ui.title: UX and UI improvements
ux-ui.description: Enhancing readability, content hierarchy, and navigation

mobile.title: Mobile versions
mobile.description: Full adaptation of the website for mobile devices

finalization.title: Project finalization
finalization.description: Delivering the site in a cohesive, completed form

absolvent.result: The changes forced by the situation brought immediate results. We managed to complete the website. Absolvent Consulting was able to welcome the second quarter of 2025 with a new showcase for its activities

video.fallback: Your browser does not support the video tag.

pasibus.heading: Burger's Job Board
pasibus.intro: Pasibus is a Polish fast food chain that has gone from being a food truck restaurant to a real competitor for foreign corporations on the local market in just a few years. It consistently pursues its goals by focusing on the continuous development of its products, restaurant chain, and employees

challenge.heading: Challenge
challenge.description: The growing Pasibus network faced the need to efficiently recruit new employees for rapidly emerging new locations across Poland. They decided to create their own job board, which would recruit candidates directly to their database

goal.heading: Goal
goal.description: Building the website architecture and wireframes, which were then passed on to the visual design team

audit.title: User needs analysis
audit.description: Understanding expectations of candidates and Pasibus HR.

ux-ui.title: Information architecture
ux-ui.description: Planning navigation, hierarchy and content categories.

finalization.title: UX wireframes
finalization.description: Preparing functional layouts without the visual layer.

pasibus.result: The result was a clear and intuitive career site structure that makes it easier for candidates to navigate job opportunities while also supporting Pasibus’s employer branding. My UX and information architecture work provided a solid foundation for the following UI design and implementation phases

video.ariaLabel: Pasibus careers UX and IA overview
video.fallback: Your browser does not support the video tag.

pharmovit.heading: Supplement store
pharmovit.intro: Pharmovit is one of the largest manufacturers and distributors of dietary supplements in Poland. Year after year, the Płock-based company is gradually expanding its product portfolio and investing in various innovations

challenge.heading: Challenge
challenge.description: Together with the Pharmovit team, we faced an ambitious task. After the company celebrated its triumphs in B2B sales and production, the idea arose to try to develop retail sales. The existing online store existed separately from the company's website, so after several meetings, we decided to consolidate the two entities

goal.heading: Goal
goal.description: Creating a new e-commerce design from scratch based on existing content that we drew from previous digital materials. In addition to UI and UX, synchronization with the warehouse system was an important aspect here

audit.title: Information architecture
audit.description: Organizing the supplement offer by form, function, and product features (e.g. clean label, gluten-free).

ux-ui.title: UX wireframes and UI design
ux-ui.description: Creating layouts for the shop, product page, blog homepage and navigation – with a focus on readability and sales-supporting interfaces.

mobile.title: Responsiveness
mobile.description: Adapting the design for desktop and mobile devices so users can comfortably browse and buy on any screen.

finalization.title: Technical support
finalization.description: One-time setup for payments and warehouse system synchronization to prepare the store for real orders.

pharmovit.result: In addition to consolidating the most important content, we achieved something that is of utmost importance for any e-commerce business. After implementing the new website, we observed an increase in visibility in browsers after a few months, which translated into higher retail sales of the company's products. Synchronization with the Subiekt GT system, on the other hand, facilitated the processing of new orders by the customer service department

video.fallback: Your browser does not support the video tag.

talentdays.heading: Blog for young talents
talentdays.intro: Talent Days is one of the largest and most important events for students during the year. Since 2013, hundreds of young talents have found their first job here during each edition. However, Talent Days is not only a career event, but also a rapidly growing website that positions itself as a job board focused on junior-level job offers and a blog that has become a collection of advice on work-related issues

challenge.heading: Challenge
challenge.description: Talent Days decided to expand its range of activities and came up with a slightly old-school idea: to build a blog that would serve as a knowledge base for young talents entering the world of careers. The previous solution was based on Wordpress using a default template, and user activity was practically non-existent

goal.heading: Goal
goal.description: Complete redesign and reengineering of solutions to build the best possible user experience

audit.title: Content structure & navigation
audit.description: Designing a clear information architecture for articles, categories, and related content.

ux-ui.title: Reading experience & accessibility
ux-ui.description: Focusing on typography, spacing, and layout to make long-form content comfortable to read.

finalization.title: Engagement & CTAs
finalization.description: Integrating newsletter, registration, and Talent Match touchpoints into the reading flow.

talentdays.result: We managed to achieve the goal we set for ourselves. We implemented a completely new product design, but most importantly, in the long term, we saw an increase in the number of visits and the time users spent on the site. A place that supports young talent has come to fruition

video.ariaLabel: Talent Days blog UI and reading experience
video.fallback: Your browser does not support the video tag.

tutlo.heading: Recommendation system
tutlo.intro: Tutlo is a Polish platform for learning English online. It is based on the freedom to choose the place and time of learning. It draws on a large database of tutors from around the world

challenge.heading: Challenge
challenge.description: Tutlo created an ambassador program that anyone who wanted to could join. We had to build a place that would gather potential candidates

goal.heading: Goal
goal.description: Building an LP that would provide knowledge to new ambassador candidates and convert them to the program

audit.title: Brief analysis & UX research
audit.description: Reviewing existing materials and defining user needs of potential partners

ux-ui.title: UX wireframes
ux-ui.description: Structuring the page to guide users smoothly from information to registration

mobile.title: UI design
mobile.description: Creating a modern, visually consistent layout aligned with Tutlo's branding

finalization.title: Developer handoff
finalization.description: Preparing assets and documentation for a smooth implementation

tutlo.result: The result is a lightweight and intuitive landing page that clearly presents the program rules, addresses key user questions, and drives them toward registration. The design supports the business goal of acquiring new partners and increasing the number of Tutlo referrals

video.ariaLabel: Tutlo recommendation system landing page overview
video.fallback: Your browser does not support the video tag.

KobuStudio.heading: Architectural Studio
KobuStudio.intro: Kobu Studio is an architectural office specializing in modern, functional spaces. The brand emphasizes aesthetics, detail, and visual consistency across its projects

challenge.heading: Challenge
challenge.description: The goal was to accurately translate the provided visual design into Framer while preserving the brand’s minimalist character and high-quality project presentation. The main challenge was precise layout implementation, maintaining typographic rhythm and proportions, optimizing large portfolio images, and ensuring smooth performance

goal.heading: Goal
goal.description: The objective was to deliver a modern, lightweight, and aesthetic website, ensure intuitive portfolio presentation, enable easy future content updates, and optimize overall performance

layout-implementation.title: Layout Implementation in Framer
layout-implementation.description: Precise translation of the provided visual design while maintaining grid structure, proportions, and typographic rhythm

responsive-setup.title: Responsive Configuration
responsive-setup.description: Adapting the layout for desktop, tablet, and mobile devices while preserving clarity and the brand’s minimalist character

performance-optimization.title: Performance Optimization
performance-optimization.description: Optimizing large portfolio images, improving loading times, and ensuring smooth overall performance

seo-setup.title: SEO Setup
seo-setup.description: Configuring heading structure, meta tags, and essential SEO parameters to support search visibility

launch.title: Testing & Launch
launch.description: Final quality checks, responsive testing, and production deployment

KobuStudio.result: The result was a clean, fast, and fully responsive website that clearly presents the studio’s portfolio and supports its positioning as a modern architectural practice

video.ariaLabel: Tutlo Lead Active landing page overview
video.fallback: Your browser does not support the video tag.

intro.heading: Who I am...
intro.paragraph1: I’ve been working in design for 8 years, specializing in Web Design and UI/UX Design. My background combines both artistic and technical skills – I hold degrees in Graphic Design and Computer Science, and I’m currently pursuing Creative Management with a specialization in UX Design. This allows me to approach projects not only from an aesthetic and functional perspective.
intro.paragraph2: Privately, I’m a true UX geek – the kind of person who tells random people about the example of Norman doors. I’m also comfortable in front of the camera and occasionally create content for social media. I value people who take action, create, and keep moving forward, because I believe growth is a continuous process.

intro.title: Not only a designer
intro.description: From time to time, I step into the role of a content creator – handling everything from concept and recording to video editing. I create short-form content for social media. It’s my way of experimenting with visuals and storytelling in a fun, dynamic format.

description.beforeLink: We use cookies to ensure that the website works properly and, with your consent, for analytics and external functions. You can change your settings in the

header.title: Cookie Policy
header.intro: On this page, we explain what cookies are, how we use them, and how you can manage your preferences. To learn how we process personal data, please visit our Privacy Policy.

section1.title: 1. What are cookies?
section1.body: Cookies are small text files saved on your device that help us ensure proper site functionality, analyze traffic, and – with your consent – personalize content.

necessary.title: Necessary
necessary.body: required for the site to function (e.g., saving cookie settings). These files are always active.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.body: help us understand how you use the site (e.g., Google Analytics).

marketing.title: Marketing
marketing.body: used to tailor content and advertising campaigns.

functional.title: External functional
functional.body: e.g., Google Maps embedded on the contact page.

section3.title: 3. Consent management
section3.browserInfo: You can also manage cookies in your browser settings and delete saved files.

googleFonts.title: Google Fonts
googleFonts.body: Loading fonts used on the site.

social.title: Social Media
social.body: Links to Instagram and LinkedIn profiles.

section5.title: 5. How to disable cookies in your browser?
section5.body: Every browser allows you to delete or block cookies. However, keep in mind that some site features may not work properly if you do so.

browsers.chrome: Chrome: Settings → Privacy and security → Cookies
browsers.firefox: Firefox: Settings → Privacy and security → Cookies and site data
browsers.safari: Safari: Preferences → Privacy → Manage website data
browsers.edge: Edge: Settings → Cookies and site permissions

feedback.loadError: We couldn't load your previous cookie settings.
feedback.acceptAll: All cookie categories have been accepted and saved.
feedback.rejectAll: All non-essential cookies have been rejected.
feedback.saved: Your cookie preferences have been saved.

footer.title: Need help?
footer.body: If you have questions about our cookie policy, please contact us or check our Privacy Policy.
